title: Portuguese Malai Nigiri Breaded Delight
description: A creative twist on traditional nigiri, combining the flavors of portuguese bread and malai for a crowd-pleasing dish
instructions: Combine yeast, water, and sugar in a bowl and let it sit for 5 minutes.
Add the bread flour, malai, salt, and vegetable oil to the bowl and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 12 equal pieces.
Shape each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk.
Place a small amount of all-purpose flour in a shallow dish and coat each disk with the flour.
Dip each flour-coated disk into the beaten eggs and then coat with sesame seeds.
Shape the coated dough into nigiri shapes and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops with the beaten eggs and sprinkle with chopped parsley and maldon sea salt.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Serve immediately on a talavera plate and enjoy!
